hero.label: Canadian non-profit
hero.heading: Sustainable, healthy eating for everyday life
hero.subtitle: We are a Canadian non-profit building open technology for the public, research, and policy so sustainable, healthy diets are easier to pursue fairly. EcoDish365 is our flagship product for better food choices year-round.

problemPreview.label: WHY THIS MATTERS
problemPreview.heading: Food shapes health, climate, and fairness. We still answer it in pieces.
problemPreview.description: Poor diets and how we produce food drive massive health burdens, environmental harm, and uneven access to trustworthy guidance. NourishSphere exists so sustainable, healthy eating is easier to understand, study, and govern.
problemPreview.stat1_description: Economic burden of poor nutrition and its cascading health and environmental impacts
problemPreview.stat2_description: Attributable to poor-quality diets, the largest modifiable risk factor for disease
problemPreview.stat3_description: Of greenhouse gas emissions come from the food system

whatWeDo.label: WHAT WE DO
whatWeDo.heading: Technology for the public, science, and policy
whatWeDo.description: We steward open digital tools so food choices, research, and governance can share a common evidence base. Our aim is better health and environmental stewardship, with attention to fair access and inclusion.
whatWeDo.individuals_description: Make day-to-day food choices with nutrition quality, environmental indicators, and health-related signals in one place.
whatWeDo.researchers_description: Advance work at the interface of nutrition and sustainability using integrated nutrient data, published scoring frameworks, and life-cycle indicators you can inspect and reproduce.
whatWeDo.policymakers_description: Ground programs and standards in disclosed methods and comparable nutrition, environment, and health views so guidance can improve quality of life and support equitable outcomes.

gap.label: THE GAP
gap.heading: Diet, environment, and health are still treated separately
gap.description: Most guidance, research, and policy traditions emphasize one pillar. Everyday people, scientists, and officials then lack a shared picture of sustainable, healthy diets, which slows progress and widens inequities. Closing that gap is NourishSphere’s mandate as an organization.
gap.nourishphere_examples: Integrated evidence for the public, science, and policy

latestNews.label: LATEST
latestNews.heading: News & Updates
latestNews.placeholder1_title: NourishSphere advances open tools for sustainable diets
latestNews.placeholder1_excerpt: The organization continues development of EcoDish365 and partnerships that link diet quality with transparent environmental indicators.
latestNews.placeholder2_title: Research at the nutrition and environment interface
latestNews.placeholder2_excerpt: Collaborating teams publish methods that connect dietary patterns, health metrics, and footprint estimates using shared, documented pipelines.
latestNews.placeholder3_excerpt: Work rooted in Canadian nutrient files and regional life-cycle practice remains open to comparison with international evidence.

donors.label: FOR FUNDERS
donors.heading: Help us scale open tools for sustainable, healthy diets
donors.description: NourishSphere is a Canadian non-profit. Grants and philanthropic partnerships speed up EcoDish365, national data onboarding with country partners, and inclusive access to integrated nutrition and environmental evidence.
donors.bullet1: EcoDish365 is designed to plug in official national dietary and nutrient databases. Canada is live with the Canadian Nutrient File from Health Canada. Ghana and Rwanda are in active partnership work with national institutions to wire in local data so their populations can benefit from the same innovation.
donors.bullet2: Support funds transparent engineering, methods, partnership capacity, and equity-focused outreach so researchers, policymakers, and the public gain from one coherent stack.
donors.bullet3: We welcome foundations, bilateral programs, corporate citizenship, and major donors who want to invest in food systems intelligence with open, documented methods.

cta.heading: Support sustainable, healthy diets in Canada and beyond
cta.description: Whether you shop for food, run studies, shape programs, or fund mission-driven technology, NourishSphere exists to put integrated evidence within reach.

footer.about_description: NourishSphere is a Canadian non-profit. We develop technology that helps people choose sustainable, healthy foods, supports research on nutrition and the environment, and informs fair, evidence-based policy.

problem.hero_heading: Sustainable, healthy diets need a fairer, shared evidence base
problem.hero_description: Across Canada and globally, what we eat drives disease risk, environmental pressure, and inequality in who gets clear advice. Institutions and markets usually treat nutrition, environmental impact, and population health as separate files. That fragmentation leaves households without coherent guidance, slows cumulative science, and invites policy that optimizes one goal while weakening another.
problem.individual_pain1_description: Diet advice, environmental claims, and clinical messages often arrive through different channels. People stitch them together alone, without a single trustworthy frame for sustainable, healthy eating. Many disengage because the signals feel contradictory or exhausting.
problem.individual_pain2_description: People believe they must choose between eating healthy OR eating sustainably. Without integrated data, people operate on misconceptions about which foods are truly optimal on both dimensions.
problem.individual_pain3_title: Hard to sustain change without holistic support
problem.individual_pain3_description: Only a small share of people maintain dietary changes long term when support stays narrow. Without links to health and environmental consequences that matter to them, motivation and follow-through fade.
problem.researcher_pain1_description: Researchers must pull from 4+ separate, unlinked databases. A Nature Food paper called this 'a fundamental barrier to operationalizing food system monitoring.'
problem.researcher_pain2_description: Each research group uses different LCA methodologies, different nutrient databases, different health models. Results across studies are non-comparable, preventing cumulative knowledge building.
problem.policymaker_pain1_title: Guidelines Built on Half the Picture
problem.policymaker_pain1_description: Dietary guidelines are developed based on nutritional adequacy without systematic environmental impact consideration. A 2024 analysis found policy implementation scores remain 'alarmingly low' for nutrition.
problem.policymaker_pain2_description: EU and Canada use incompatible data systems (HSR vs. Nutri-Score vs. FCS). Environmental LCA is regionalized differently. International collaboration remains aspirational.
problem.synthesis_heading: An organization-wide challenge, not a feature list
problem.synthesis_description: NourishSphere is a Canadian non-profit working on this systems problem: transparent methods, open technology, and partnerships so sustainable, healthy diets are easier to pursue in daily life, in research, and in policy with equity and inclusion. Digital products are one way we deliver on that mandate. They are not the reason the organization exists.
problem.synthesis_insight: When communities, scientists, and governments can refer to the same documented links among diet, health, and environment, trade-offs become legible and accountability improves.

mission.label: OUR MISSION
mission.heading: Sustainable, healthy diets through open technology
mission.mission_statement: NourishSphere promotes sustainable, healthy diet patterns through technology for consumers, researchers, and policymakers. We focus on transparent methods so choices, science, and governance can improve quality of life and advance equity and inclusion.
mission.vision_statement: Communities across Canada and elsewhere have trustworthy tools to align healthy eating with environmental stewardship. Benefits reach people fairly, including those who have been underserved by fragmented food information.
mission.architecture_description: NourishSphere is the Canadian NGO. EcoDish365 is our flagship digital product design for sustainable, healthy food choices 365 days a year.
mission.org_role: The registered Canadian non-profit: mission, fundraising, partnerships, research, and policy engagement.
mission.product_role: The public digital experience: meal workflows, nutrient and footprint indicators, and documentation for everyday sustainable, healthy eating.
mission.history_description: NourishSphere grew out of McGill University research. The team found that informed food choices needed tools that did not exist. Evidence lived in separate nutrient databases, life-cycle inventories, and health models, without a shared, actionable framework.
mission.value1_description: Our methodologies are transparent, peer-reviewed, and publicly documented.
mission.value2_description: English and French are built in for Canada. We pay attention to equitable access across regions and communities.
mission.value3_description: Every claim is backed by validated data, peer-reviewed scoring systems, and reproducible methods.
mission.value4_description: GDPR and Quebec Bill 25 compliant from day one. No advertising, no data selling.

team.label: OUR TEAM
team.heading: Led by Science. Driven by Impact.
team.description: NourishSphere is led by a multidisciplinary team with deep expertise in nutritional science, environmental assessment, and public health policy.
team.founder_bio: Dual-PhD McGill professor specializing in nutritional epidemiology and environmental life-cycle assessment. Over 15 years of research at the intersection of diet, health outcomes, and environmental sustainability.
team.advisory_description: Our advisory board brings together leaders in nutrition science, environmental policy, digital health, and non-profit governance.
team.join_heading: Help Build the Future of Food Intelligence
team.join_description: We're looking for passionate researchers, developers, and communicators who want to make a difference at the intersection of nutrition and sustainability.

methodology.label: METHODOLOGY
methodology.heading: The Science Behind NourishSphere
methodology.description: How scores, footprints, and official nutrient data fit together in EcoDish365, the flagship product NourishSphere stewards for integrated food intelligence. The architecture is built so national dietary composition tables can be swapped in or extended as each country connects its authoritative data.
methodology.hsr_description: A front-of-pack labeling system that rates the overall nutritional profile of packaged food on a scale of 0.5 to 5 stars. Considers energy, saturated fat, sodium, sugar, protein, fiber, and fruit/vegetable content.
methodology.fcs_description: Developed at Tufts University, FCS scores foods from 1 to 100 using 54 attributes across 9 domains, including nutrients, processing, vitamins, and phytochemicals.
methodology.hefi_description: Aligned with Canada's Food Guide, HEFI measures dietary quality based on whole grains, protein foods, fruits & vegetables, unsaturated fats, and limits on sodium, sugars, and saturated fats.
methodology.heni_description: A DALY-based metric for the health burden of dietary choices. It relates intake to disability-adjusted life years and clinical outcomes.
methodology.lca_description: Using ReCiPe 2016 midpoint with Canadian regionalization, NourishSphere estimates environmental footprints across 18 categories (e.g., climate, water, land, ecotoxicity). Results can be expressed in CAD for comparison.
methodology.cnf_description: Created and maintained by Health Canada, the CNF is the official reference for nutrient values across 5,000+ foods commonly consumed in Canada. It is the live national layer in EcoDish365 today.
methodology.gbd_description: The Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation's database linking dietary risk factors to health outcomes worldwide.
methodology.integration_heading: Nutrition, environment, and health in one frame
methodology.integration_description: These indices and the national nutrient layer sit with ReCiPe-style footprint results. Users can read diet quality, population-health signals, and environmental burdens without changing tools. As additional countries plug in official tables, the same integration model travels with them.
methodology.national_heading: Ready for official dietary data in more countries
methodology.national_description: EcoDish365 is designed so each deployment can use its country's national nutrient or food composition database behind the same scoring, health, and footprint stack.
methodology.national_canada_description: The Canadian Nutrient File from Health Canada is connected today. It supplies authoritative composition data for meal and food workflows nationwide.
methodology.national_ghana_description: Together with institutions in Ghana, we are working to integrate Ghana's national dietary data so communities and programs there can use EcoDish365 with locally relevant foods and nutrients.
methodology.national_rwanda_description: Parallel collaboration with Rwandan partners is underway to plug Rwanda's national database into the stack, supporting impact that reflects local diets and policy needs.

contact.label: GET IN TOUCH
contact.heading: Let's Work Together
contact.description: Reach out for research collaboration, partnership, media, philanthropy, or institutional funding. Ask how NourishSphere and EcoDish365 can support your goals, including national data integrations.
contact.form_success: Thank you! Your message has been sent. We'll get back to you within 48 hours.

platform.hero_heading: EcoDish365: sustainable, healthy choices every day
platform.hero_description: EcoDish365 is NourishSphere's flagship digital product. It is architected to plug in official national dietary and nutrient databases alongside the same scoring, health, and environmental stack. Canada uses Health Canada's Canadian Nutrient File today; Ghana and Rwanda are advancing with partner institutions. The design is ready for impact as more countries connect their authoritative data.
platform.audiences_description: EcoDish365 combines nutrition, environment, and health analytics for personal use, research, and policy analysis.
platform.audience1_description: Make informed food choices for health and the planet. Explore nutrition and environmental trade-offs through meal-oriented workflows and clear indicators.
platform.audience2_description: Work at the intersection of nutrition, health, and environment using the CNF, validated scoring systems (HSR, FCS, HEFI, HENI), and LCA-based indicators.
platform.audience3_description: Support decisions on nutrition, health, and the environment, including guidelines, procurement, and sustainability programs, with transparent and reproducible methods.
platform.meal_integration_heading: From foods and meals to scores and impacts
platform.meal_integration_description: EcoDish365 describes meal creation and sharing where nutrition scores (HSR, FCS, HEFI, HENI), environmental indicators, and monetized CAD impacts appear together.
platform.deploy1_description: Multiple validated indices side by side so meals and foods can be compared on diet quality and health-related impact, not a single opaque score.
platform.deploy2_description: ReCiPe 2016 midpoint impacts per 100 kcal with Canadian regionalization, optional monetization in Canadian dollars for communication and comparison.
platform.deploy3_description: Build meals and view nutrition scores, environmental indicators, and monetized costs together, as in the public EcoDish365 product.
platform.impact_dietary_desc: Foods in Canada's nutrient database as referenced on EcoDish365.
platform.impact_ghg_desc: Nutrients tracked in the CNF integration described on the product site.
platform.impact_waste_desc: ReCiPe 2016 midpoint categories (per 100 kcal) on EcoDish365.
platform.impact_productivity_desc: HSR, FCS, HEFI, and HENI integrated in the public platform description.
platform.cta_heading: Try the product, partner, or support the mission
platform.cta_description: Open the live EcoDish365 application for hands-on tools and documentation. Contact NourishSphere for partnerships, research, policy conversations, or philanthropic and institutional funding.
platform.individuals_tools_heading: What you can do in EcoDish365
platform.individuals_feature1_description: Build meals and instantly see nutrition scores (HSR, FCS, HEFI, HENI) plus environmental indicators and monetized costs in CAD.
platform.individuals_feature2_description: Search and compare foods in Canada's nutrient file (5,000+ foods, 150+ nutrients), using the same scoring and footprint logic as meals.
platform.individuals_feature3_description: See dietary quality and DALY-style health signals alongside ReCiPe-based environmental indicators so choices are not narrowed to calories alone.
platform.individuals_stats_description: Figures match EcoDish365's description of its integrated nutrient file, LCA scope, and score families.
platform.individuals_hero_heading: Personal food choices with full context
platform.individuals_hero_description: Make informed food choices for health and the environment. Nutrition indices, environmental indicators, and meal workflows appear in one place.
platform.individuals_meal_section_description: EcoDish365 focuses on meals where nutrition scores and environmental indicators appear together instead of in separate tools.
platform.individuals_privacy_heading: Read the policies on the live site
platform.individuals_privacy_description: How accounts, cookies, and data are handled is defined in EcoDish365's Privacy Policy and Terms. NourishSphere does not claim zero-knowledge or special cryptography on your behalf; use the live product documentation for the authoritative wording.
platform.individuals_cta_description: Open EcoDish365 to use meal tools and documentation, or contact us for questions about NourishSphere's research and partnerships.
platform.researchers_hero_heading: Research at the nutrition and environment interface
platform.researchers_hero_description: The open EcoDish365 stack on GitHub combines Canadian nutrient data, ReCiPe-based LCA, monetization, and published indices. The design favors reproducible analysis over opaque models.
platform.researchers_pipeline_heading: What the codebase is built around
platform.researchers_pipeline_description: The documented front end and API integrate national-layer nutrient data (Canada live; additional countries in partnership), scoring pipelines (HSR, FCS, HEFI, HENI), and environmental assessment in line with the public EcoDish365 description.
platform.researchers_feature1_description: Structured access plus search and comparison patterns suitable for meal and diet-level studies tied to official composition data.
platform.researchers_feature2_description: ReCiPe 2016 midpoint with regionalization and CAD monetization for communicating environmental trade-offs alongside diet metrics.
platform.researchers_feature3_description: Code and documentation for HSR, Food Compass 2.0, HEFI-2019-style logic, and HENI-style health impact estimates, with references to published methods.
platform.researchers_feature4_description: Inspect and fork the project at github.com/Amankrah/ecodish365; deployment notes live in the repository rather than proprietary middleware claims.
platform.researchers_data_description: Headline numbers match EcoDish365's public description of the CNF, LCA scope, and score families.
platform.researchers_integrations_description: Labels match EcoDish365 and the repository. They are not generic EU data-space program names.
platform.researchers_cta_description: Review the code and methodology briefs, then contact NourishSphere or the EcoDish365 maintainers for integration and research questions.
platform.policymakers_hero_description: EcoDish365 provides structured nutrient data, standardized footprint indicators, and established diet-quality and health-impact scores. These support guidelines, procurement rules, and reporting when methods are disclosed.
platform.policymakers_output1_description: Align procurement or education materials using the same CNF-backed nutrient fields and ReCiPe-based indicators described publicly on EcoDish365.
platform.policymakers_output2_description: Each of HSR, FCS, HEFI, and HENI has a traceable methodology, which helps technical review.
platform.policymakers_output3_description: Optional dollar valuation of environmental burdens to complement physical indicators, matching the product's stated monetization feature.
platform.policymakers_output4_description: Use EcoDish365 documentation and the GitHub repository to verify assumptions before adopting results in policy memos or standards work.
platform.policymakers_section_case_description: When meals are the unit of intervention (schools, workplace cafeterias, hospitals), EcoDish365 meal workflows show joint nutrition and environmental metrics.
platform.policymakers_scope_description: This site does not list EU ministry partnerships or audited pilot percentages. Validate any deployment claims directly with EcoDish365 and published evaluations.
platform.policymakers_cta_description: Use EcoDish365 for hands-on exploration, read the methodology section on the EcoDish365 platform page for technical framing, and contact us for advisory conversations grounded in disclosed methods.

support.meta_description: One-page overview for donors and funders: NourishSphere, EcoDish365, pluggable national dietary data (Canada live; Ghana and Rwanda in partnership), and how to support the mission.
support.print_hint: Use your browser print dialog to save this page as a PDF.
support.doc_subtitle: Sustainable, healthy diets through open technology
support.summary_p1: NourishSphere is a Canadian non-profit promoting sustainable, healthy diet patterns through technology for consumers, researchers, and policymakers. We focus on transparent methods, equity, and inclusion so food choices, science, and governance can share a clearer evidence base.
support.summary_p2: EcoDish365 is our flagship digital product. It integrates nutrition, environmental impact, and health-related signals in one stack so users are not forced to reconcile disconnected apps and spreadsheets.
support.opportunity_p: Diet quality, environmental pressure from food systems, and uneven access to trustworthy guidance remain global challenges. Institutions often treat nutrition, environment, and population health as separate tracks. Philanthropic and institutional funding helps close that gap with open, documented tools and country partnerships rather than closed, vendor-locked platforms.
support.data_heading: National data, built to plug in
support.data_intro: EcoDish365 is designed so each country can connect its official national nutrient or food composition database behind the same scoring, health, and footprint logic. That supports fair, local relevance as the program grows.
support.data_canada: The Canadian Nutrient File from Health Canada is integrated today for foods commonly consumed in Canada.
support.data_ghana: We are working with national institutions to integrate Ghana's dietary composition data so local populations and programs can benefit from the same innovation.
support.data_rwanda: Parallel partnership work is underway to connect Rwanda's national database into the stack for impact grounded in local diets and policy needs.
support.use1: Engineering and quality assurance for secure, reliable public tools and documentation.
support.use2: National data onboarding, validation with partner institutions, and methods transparency.
support.use3: Community, researcher, and policy engagement that prioritizes inclusion and responsible rollout.
support.use4: Sustainability of the non-profit as steward of open pipelines rather than advertising or sale of user data.
support.fit_intro: Organizations and individuals who share the mission and expect accountable, evidence-aligned reporting.
support.fit1: Private and family foundations, donor-advised funds, and strategic philanthropies.
support.fit2: Public and bilateral programs that fund global health, climate-smart food systems, or digital public goods.
support.fit3: Corporate citizenship and sustainability teams seeking structured, open-impact partnerships.
support.fit4: Major individual donors who want to back nutrition and environmental integration with public benefit.
support.due_p: NourishSphere operates as a Canadian non-profit with published methodological references for EcoDish365 and open codebase visibility where applicable. We are glad to share more for due diligence, including partnership status and workplan detail under a confidentiality agreement when appropriate.
support.cta_body: Contact us and select the inquiry type Philanthropy & institutional funding. Briefly describe your institution or program, timeline, and interests (for example national data expansion, research collaboration, or general operating support).
